steps.title: Spot the causal signal
steps.content: The first sentence describes Kelley's extensive research and specialization. The second describes colleagues regarding her book as reliable. The transition must show that the second outcome results from the first cause—her research and expertise led to professional trust.

steps.title: Match the causal relationship
steps.content:
'Consequently' shows the causal relationship: extensive research resulted in colleagues regarding the book as reliable.

The other three all fail to show causation: 'Likewise' indicates a parallel relationship, 'Next' marks only sequence, and 'Nevertheless' creates a false contrast.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
